resources.string:
You’re on the home screen for Live Vocoder on Android (7.0).

The live vocoder (mic + carrier + effects) still runs on Windows, Linux, and macOS — not inside this APK yet.

Use the buttons: get the newest APK from GitHub, or open releases for the Windows installer. Carrier files and virtual-audio notes are under Full help.

This Android package matches the desktop release line (7.0). The full real-time vocoder stack (SDL, PortAudio, FFTW) runs on Windows, Linux, and macOS. An on-device audio engine for Android is not in this build yet.

In the Windows/Linux/macOS app, put these mono 48 kHz .f32 files in Documents/LiveVocoderCarriers (or use the shortcut chips):

• Gangstas Paradise (Instrumental) (1).f32
• Hide (CS01 Version).f32
• Rick Astley - Never Gonna Give You Up (Official Video) (4K Remaster).f32

Android has no system-wide “virtual cable” like VB‑Cable on Windows. Apps use the mic (AudioRecord) and speakers (AudioTrack) or low-latency AAudio/Oboe.

Android 10+ can capture other apps’ playback only when those apps allow it (AudioPlaybackCapture), often together with screen capture — not a generic loopback for every stream.

For routing to another device, use USB audio or Bluetooth. A future Live Vocoder engine here would use the mic permission and in-app processing — not a installable virtual driver.
Version %1$s is on GitHub (you have %2$s). Download and install the APK?
Latest GitHub release has no .apk file. Open the releases page in the browser?
Allow installing updates for this app (unknown sources), then try again.
